notes.title: ☾Κλos ♃i
notes.markdown:
Today's Name:
# ☾Κλos ♃i

>Note: it is pronounced \[kəlɪstoʊs joʊviː] or \[kəlɪstoʊs dʒoʊviː] but *please* use the alchemical symbols if you are writing. 

☾Κλos -> \[kəlɪstoʊs]      
♃i -> \[joviː] 

> [!error] If you are in a hurry
> 
> Just use "☾Κλe" \[kəlɪsteɪ] to refer to me!

***
elsewise, if you want to take the time to learn Latin<sup>1</sup> cases:
## Talking about me (subject) \[-oʊs] 
*Wow, ☾Κλos always remembers to log out of the database!*
## Talking about me (direct object) \[-əm]
*lowkey, It's giving ☾Κλum*
## Talking about me (indirect object) \[-oʊ]
*Anika<sup>2</sup>, can you stop being so mean to ☾Κλo?*
## Talking to me \[-eɪ]
*☾Κλe, you should tell me more about the moons of Jupiter!*
## Talking about a thing which belongs to me \[-i]
*Guys, has anyone seen ☾Κλi Celsium?* 

***

><sup>1</sup>Nominative case is from Greek because I felt like it, rest is Latin. 

> <sup>2</sup>Depending on how she feels about it, perhaps this should be "Anikē, ..."
> Update: *Do **not** conjugate Anika's name.*
***
# Some Cool Facts about Calisto (moon):

- I mean just look at how cool it is 

![[Pasted image 20251001144542.png]]

Image Credits: https://solarsystem.nasa.gov/moons/jupiter-moons/callisto/galleries

***

notes.title: ☾Γµ ♃i
notes.markdown:
Today's Name:
# ☾Γµ ♃i

Note: it is pronounced \[gænɪmiːd joʊviː] or \[gænɪmiːd dʒoʊviː] but *please* use the alchemical symbols if you are writing. 

☾Γµ -> \[gænɪmiːd]      
♃i -> \[joviː] 

***
### IF YOU ARE IN A HURRY, JUST USE "☾Γµe" TO REFER TO ME \[gænɪmiːdeɪ]
***

elsewise, if you want to take the time to learn Latin cases:
## Talking about me (subject) \[-∅]<sup>1</sup> 
*Wow, ☾Γµ always remembers to log out of the database!*
## Talking about me (object) \[-əm]
*Anika<sup>2</sup>, can you stop being so mean to ☾Γµum?*
## Talking to me \[-e]
*☾Γµe, you should tell me more about the moons of Jupiter!*
## Talking about a thing which belongs to me \[-i]
*Guys, has anyone seen ☾Γµi Celsium?* 

***

>I have opted to go for no case marking in the nominative case as a style preference. If you want to be pedantic, you can use ☾Γµus. 

> <sup>2</sup>Depending on how she feels about it, perhaps this should be "Anikē, ..."
***
#### There are two more Latin cases but lowkey English prepositions makes them redundant and they're kinda lame anyway. But you can use ☾Γµo for Dative and Accusative if you want. 

***
# Some Cool Facts about Ganymede (moon):

- Largest moon in the solar system 
- Larger than Mercury
- Rocky and Water Icy 
- Water frost polar aps 
- Iron-Sulphide and Iron core 
- Solar System body with the lowest moment of Inertia: 0.31

notes.title: Will be home
notes.markdown:
> [!info]
> I'm still making this website. None of these hyperlinks work. Come back later. 
## Welcome to my website

It's part interactive résumé,
part online storage where I can dump completed projects,
and part blog. 

***
## [[Flight Control Algorithms]]
All the cool stuff from when I was working with drones. 
## [[Other Work Experience]]
Internships in Operations Research, AI Research, and Teaching
## [[Linguistics]]
My work at the Language Acquisition Lab at the University of Maryland. 
\+ Experience being the treasurer for The Linguistics Club @ UMD. 
## [[Personal Projects]]
For those quick python programs you write when you come up with a funny idea. Also that one Hackathon I did that one time. 
## [[3D Modeling]]
Blend files to CAD files, everything 3d goes here. 

***

## About me:
I am a Computer Science student with a minor in Linguistics at the University of Maryland, College Park.
